slides.title: Tired by 3pm? Start with *morning light*.
slides.sub: The fix for afternoon crashes might be the first 10 minutes of your day.

slides.title: Coffee before daylight
slides.body: Caffeine on a dark brain borrows energy you haven't made yet. *Light comes first* — then coffee.

slides.title: Get outside early
slides.body: Aim for *10 minutes of daylight* within an hour of waking — no sunglasses, no window glass.

slides.title: It sets your clock
slides.body: Morning light anchors your circadian rhythm, which *can support* steadier energy and easier sleep that night.

slides.title: Still go out
slides.body: Overcast morning light is still *10x brighter* than indoor lighting. Step out anyway.

slides.title: Pair it with a walk
slides.body: Walk while you get your light. *Two wins at once* — movement and a reset body clock.

slides.title: Make morning light a *daily streak*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your light, sleep, and steps into one daily Health Score — so you can see the habit pay off.

caption:
If you're wired at night and dead by 3pm, your morning might be the problem. ☀️

Get 10 minutes of daylight before coffee, skip the sunglasses, pair it with a short walk. Small habit, big shift. Save this for tomorrow morning. 🔖

Vibe Health scores your light, sleep, steps and food into one number so you can watch it climb → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Morning Light Beats Coffee for Energy

bg_prompts.cover: Black-and-white cinematic editorial photograph. Dramatic low-key lighting, deep shadows, dark charcoal background. Realistic texture, shallow depth of field. A person stepping onto a balcony at sunrise, eyes closed and face turned up to the early light, soft glow rimming one side.
